title: Accelerating Rotarod: Motor Coordination and Balance Assessment in Mice
author: Sarah van Veen

steps.title: Training phase Place each mouse on...
steps.instruction: Training phase Place each mouse on the rotating rod already set to constant speed (5 rpm). Allow the mouse to walk on the rod for 2 min. If the mouse drops during training, place it back on the rod (up to 3 times). This familiarizes the animal with the apparatus. Return mouse to home cage after training.

steps.title: Test trials - setup Place all...
steps.instruction: Test trials - setup Place all mice onto the rotating rod at 5 rpm, one at a time, ensuring all four paws are gripping mid-rod. Once all mice are positioned, start the acceleration protocol. The rod accelerates from 5 rpm to 40 rpm over 5 min (300 s). Record latency to drop (in seconds) for each mouse. Maximum trial duration is 300 s. If a mouse clings to the rod and does not walk on it, observe for a maximum of 3 full rotations to allow it to resume walking. If the mouse continues clinging after 3 rotations, remove it from the apparatus and record the time at that point as the latency.

steps.title: Inter-trial rest (10 min) Return each...
steps.instruction: Inter-trial rest (10 min) Return each mouse to its home cage for a 10-min inter-trial interval. Repeat Steps 2 and 3 for trials 2, 3, and 4.

steps.title: Complete all 4 trials Each mouse...
steps.instruction: Complete all 4 trials Each mouse completes 4 test trials with 10-min inter-trial intervals. Record latency to fall for each trial. Calculate the mean latency across all 4 trials per animal.

steps.title: Data analysis Express performance as mean...
steps.instruction: Data analysis Express performance as mean latency to fall ± SEM across the 4 test trials per animal. Compare WT vs KO using appropriate statistics.
